task_completion.description: Thunderbird application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Thunderbird mail/feed client window is open on the screen.

task_completion.description: A Feed Account named 'Market Intelligence' is created.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Market Intelligence' appears in the left-hand folder pane of Thunderbird, designated as a feed account (often with an RSS icon).

task_completion.description: The Feed Subscriptions dialog or tab is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A window or tab titled 'Feed Subscriptions' (or similar Thunderbird feed management interface) is open.

task_completion.description: The correct internal corporate RSS feed URL is entered.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The URL 'http://localhost:8080/industry_news.xml' is visible in the Feed URL input field or listed as a subscribed feed URL.

task_completion.description: The feed is successfully subscribed to and retrieves the expected article.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The article titled 'Globex Corp Announces Q3 Results' is visible in the Thunderbird message list pane under the 'Market Intelligence' account.

integrity.description: The agent used the Thunderbird graphical user interface to configure the feed.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with Thunderbird menus, dialogs, and settings rather than terminal windows editing configuration files.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply open the XML file in a browser or text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The article 'Globex Corp Announces Q3 Results' is rendered within the Thunderbird application interface, not as raw XML text in a web browser, terminal, or text editor.

integrity.description: The agent did not use curl, wget, or python scripts to fetch the feed outside of Thunderbird.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows showing commands like 'curl http://localhost:8080/industry_news.xml' being used to satisfy the article retrieval requirement.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected feed account name is 'Market Intelligence'. The target feed URL is 'http://localhost:8080/industry_news.xml'. A successful subscription will result in Thunderbird displaying an article titled 'Globex Corp Announces Q3 Results'.
